reference: Our thirteen-month-old just received her first-year vaccinations on Tuesday. They administered the whooping cough vaccine and the measles, mumps, and rubella vaccine, along with the diphtheria, tetanus, and pertussis vaccine. She developed a cough the next day, and it has progressively worsened. What are some signs that we need to get her checked out?
text: Our fourteen-month-old just received her first-year vaccinations on Tuesday. They administered the whooping cough vaccine and the measles, mumps, and rubella vaccine, along with the diphtheria, tetanus, and pertussis vaccine. She developed a cough the next day, and it has progressively worsened. What are some signs that we need to get her checked out?
